subject: Databases
title: Many-to-Many with Junction Tables
description: A fast breakdown of Many-to-Many with Junction Tables for builders.

hook.text: A student takes many courses. A course has many students. How do you model that? A junction table.

buildup.text: Create a student_courses table with student_id and course_id. Each row is one enrollment.

discovery.text: Join three tables: students → student_courses → courses. The junction table links the two sides.

twist.text: Junction tables can carry extra data: enrolled_at, grade, status. They're more than just links.

climax.text: Tags, permissions, and playlists all use junction tables. The pattern is everywhere.

punchline.text: Many-to-many needs a junction table. It's the bridge.

quiz.question: Why do many-to-many relationships require a junction table?
options: Neither table can hold multiple foreign keys for the other, so a linking table is needed
Databases can only join two tables at a time
It's a performance optimization required by SQL
